# Methods

## Holographic Observables

The project will lean first on observables that already appear in the anchor literature: large-subsystem entanglement entropy, coarse-grained CFT observables, and any explicit dictionary relating interior or final-state data to boundary quantities.

## Euclidean Path Integral and Analytic Continuation

Several anchor constructions begin from a Euclidean saddle and obtain either a cosmology or a wormhole by analytic continuation. This means any singularity statement has to track which continuation is being used and whether the claim is geometric, informational, or observer-dependent.

## Doubly-Holographic / BCFT Reasoning

The magnetic braneworld construction explicitly uses BCFT language on a strip. This is methodologically important because it turns some otherwise qualitative statements about cosmology into a sharper microscopic description.

## Final-State Projection

The closed-universe paper introduces a final-state projection language for the no-direct-access regime. This method may become the cleanest way to compare "the CFT determines the universe" with "the CFT reconstructs the universe."

## Comparative Diagnostic Method

The main bootstrap method is a controlled comparison across papers. Every claim will be tagged by:

- geometric statement,
- boundary observable,
- entanglement role,
- access/reconstruction claim,
- singularity interpretation,
- unresolved loophole.
